rag_rules: Fix AGREE syntax errors by correcting invalid expressions or missing declarations in the current component's annex.
Ensure generated AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and valid types from the component scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of referenced features.
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and avoid introducing undefined or unqualified references.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes; avoid referencing implementation-only paths in type contracts.
When fixing diagnostics, prioritize minimal repairs that preserve existing architecture and avoid introducing new dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must follow valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, equations, and assignments.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare explicit types (int, real, bool) and reference only visible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL property references that are not present in the current model scope.
Do not attempt to reference implementation-only subcomponent ports or paths in a component type annex.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in system vs system implementation
Component type vs implementation scope boundaries for feature visibility
